explicit_approval_required: Any setup revision, invariant, risk, model, fallback, budget, path, dependency, network, deployment, credential, data, or promotion boundary change.
Consuming feedback that conflicts with the frozen brief.
may_proceed_without_approval: Create and improve in-scope Loop Artifacts, run frozen local commands, merge valid fragments, rebuild the in-progress Viewer, and record evidence within the approved generated root.
Validate, disposition, and consume immutable human feedback only when it remains within frozen optimization variables and all exact verbatim linkage requirements pass.
pause_points: After finishing the current atomic write/test/merge, rebuild viewer.html, checkpoint any downloaded intake JSON unchanged, and pause before the next Loop Prompt.
Pause after the canonical final Evidence Gate passes and before deterministic promotion into examples/**.

During later attended promotion, create one standalone maintained Example Prompt from the frozen contract and use a narrow tested promotion command to copy only the already-gated snapshot, sanitize paths, record source setup hashes, apply Example provenance, generate the Example README, refresh hashes, rebuild deterministically, rerun Navigation Evidence, rerun the final Evidence Gate, and atomically add only examples/accessible-mobile-checkout.

pause_conditions: A requested write, command, model, fallback, path, budget, or behavior is outside this approved brief.
Any action requires network access, browsing, dependency installation, deployment, telemetry, credentials, external users, real or sensitive data, or an external service.
A model is unavailable and no listed fallback preserves generator-judge role independence.

The early npm test --prefix references/navigation_judge preflight fails, references/navigation_judge/node_modules is absent, or cached Chromium cannot launch; dependency installation is prohibited unattended.
A long-running command lacks a recorded handle or heartbeat, or its state cannot be distinguished from still running; never rerun it while it may be live.
A Windows scratch path contains an 8.3 component such as CHRISA~1 or is outside target.scratch_root.
The frozen harness, fixture, brief, setup Prompt, approval binding, or parent evidence would need mutation.
Human feedback arrives: finish the current atomic operation, merge its fragment, rebuild the in-progress Viewer, checkpoint immutable intake, then pause before issuing the next Loop Prompt.
Promotion into examples/** or any maintained repository edit is requested; that is a separate attended phase after the canonical Experiment passes.
prohibited_actions: Do not modify the approved brief, Prompt, approval, frozen harness after generators start, existing Examples, repository source, tests, docs, workflows, or git history during the Experiment.

Do not deploy, publish, create telemetry, access credentials, use real or sensitive data, contact external users, make runtime network requests, browse prior art, or call unapproved external services.

Do not install or update dependencies, browsers, lockfiles, packages, fonts, images, or libraries during unattended execution. The attended-preflight references/navigation_judge/node_modules must remain read-only through the run and be deleted only before the later final commit.
Do not silently substitute a model, weaken a gate, rewrite failed evidence, hand-edit generated evidence, delete failed Loops, or promote a candidate whose objective gate failed.
Do not rerun a live or indeterminate command; use its recorded handle and heartbeat to inspect it.
Do not commit, open a pull request, merge, or promote the Example during unattended execution.
stop_conditions: Stop successfully only after three primary Tracks have at least two Loops each, synthesis has at least two Loops, all blocking gates pass, blind judging is complete with dissent preserved, synthesis has no objective regression, the final Viewer rebuild is deterministic, Navigation Evidence passes, and the final Evidence Gate passes.
Stop early after the planned eight Loops only when all success conditions already pass and both synthesis Loops establish convergence.
Stop as failed when the eleven-Loop or 240-minute hard cap is reached without all blocking completion conditions.
Stop as failed after two consecutive repair attempts reproduce the same blocking defect without a new evidence-backed hypothesis.

categories.assessment: Repeated activation, invalid synthetic payment data, accidental back navigation, and premature submission are foreseeable.

scenarios.context: A mobile user is uncertain whether activation succeeded.
scenarios.evidence_plan: The frozen browser harness attempts invalid and repeated submission and requires one synthetic confirmation with no external effect.
scenarios.material_friction: Duplicate or premature confirmation would undermine safety and trust.
scenarios.operation: Activate Place order repeatedly or while invalid.
scenarios.rationale: Completion must be explicit, single, and synthetic.

categories.assessment: Accessibility behavior must not weaken payment privacy, validation, confirmation, or transaction correctness.

scenarios.context: A cross-disability user relies on programmatic labels, status, focus, and error relationships.
scenarios.evidence_plan: The frozen harness verifies names, roles, labels, descriptions, focus targets, status regions, and the confirmation state.
scenarios.material_friction: A visually apparent but semantically hidden safety message or confirmation can cause an unsafe action.
scenarios.operation: Complete and correct checkout with assistive semantics and keyboard input.
scenarios.rationale: Safety-critical validation and status must be available through the same accessible interaction path.

categories.assessment: Screen-reader semantics, keyboard operation, visible focus, contrast, reduced motion, and accessible error handling are central.

scenarios.context: Blind, low-vision, motor-constrained, reduced-motion, or cognitively burdened mobile use.
scenarios.evidence_plan: Browser assertions cover semantics, keyboard path, focus indication, computed contrast, and reduced-motion behavior; blind judges assess remaining friction.
scenarios.material_friction: Unlabelled controls, weak focus, poor contrast, inaccessible status, or motion can block checkout.
scenarios.operation: Navigate, understand, enter data, recover, review, and confirm.
scenarios.rationale: Objectively testable accessibility requirements are blocking correctness, while residual experience quality remains qualitative.

categories.assessment: Users must immediately understand the task, required information, progress, totals, and primary action.

scenarios.context: First use on a narrow mobile viewport.
scenarios.evidence_plan: Each Loop provides mobile screenshots and an operable candidate; blind judges score discoverability with cited Artifact evidence.
scenarios.material_friction: Hidden requirements or unclear next actions increase abandonment and cognitive effort.
scenarios.operation: Orient to checkout and identify the next useful action.
scenarios.rationale: Discoverability quality is contextual and should be judged from rendered evidence, not reduced to a numeric geometry gate.

categories.assessment: Landmarks, headings, step or section movement, back/edit actions, focus order, and review access must remain coherent.

scenarios.evidence_plan: The frozen task runner uses Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, and Space to complete and edit the checkout without pointer-only actions.
scenarios.material_friction: Focus loss, traps, ambiguous back actions, or hidden review state can block completion.
scenarios.operation: Move through every checkout task and return to edit earlier information.
scenarios.rationale: A complete keyboard path is objectively testable and required for correctness.

categories.assessment: The checkout should minimize repeated entry and unnecessary decisions without omitting required information.

scenarios.context: A user on a small touch device or switch/keyboard input.
scenarios.evidence_plan: The objective runner records focusable-control and interaction counts; blind judges interpret those metrics with the rendered flow.
scenarios.material_friction: Redundant fields, excessive traversal, or unclear formats raise effort and error risk.
scenarios.operation: Enter contact, delivery, shipping, and synthetic payment details.
scenarios.rationale: Input burden depends on coherent flow and context, not a single universal field-count threshold.

categories.assessment: Invalid or missing values must be prevented, explained, focused, preserved, and correctable.

scenarios.context: A user makes multiple mistakes during mobile checkout.
scenarios.evidence_plan: The frozen harness checks error summary, field associations, focus, value preservation, correction, and successful resubmission.
scenarios.material_friction: Unclear summaries, lost values, or inaccessible field errors create repeated failure.
scenarios.operation: Submit empty and invalid fields, then correct them without losing valid work.
scenarios.rationale: The canonical invalid-to-valid recovery journey is objectively testable.

categories.assessment: Progress, saved state, validation, submission, and confirmation must be timely and programmatically exposed.

scenarios.context: Visual and nonvisual mobile use under uncertainty.
scenarios.evidence_plan: The harness records status-region assertions; judges assess clarity, timing, and confidence from task evidence.
scenarios.material_friction: Missing or inaccessible status causes repeated activation and loss of confidence.
scenarios.operation: Advance, save, submit, and observe confirmation.
scenarios.rationale: Programmatic status presence is objective, while clarity and confidence are qualitative.

categories.assessment: Controls must remain operable, legible, and unclipped across representative mobile viewports and touch interaction.

scenarios.context: Portrait mobile use with touch, keyboard, or switch input.
scenarios.evidence_plan: The harness measures every interactive target and viewport overflow; judges separately assess responsive touch ergonomics without inventing numeric comfort gates.
scenarios.material_friction: Clipping, horizontal scrolling, tiny targets, or crowded controls can block or degrade operation.
scenarios.operation: Operate every checkout control at 320, 360, and 390 CSS-pixel widths.
scenarios.rationale: Minimum target and no-overflow checks are frozen technical compatibility gates; overall touch comfort remains qualitative.

categories.assessment: Non-sensitive progress should survive reload while synthetic payment secrets never persist.

scenarios.context: A mobile session is interrupted before payment completion.
scenarios.evidence_plan: The harness reloads mid-flow, requires restoration of approved non-sensitive values and state, and asserts card number, expiry, and security code are absent from storage.
scenarios.material_friction: Losing safe progress causes abandonment; persisting payment data creates a privacy defect.
scenarios.operation: Reload after partial contact, address, and shipping entry, then resume.
scenarios.rationale: The exact safe-resume behavior is objectively testable.

categories.assessment: A synthetic short submission delay must expose a stable busy state without duplicate action or misleading completion.

scenarios.context: A local prototype simulates bounded processing without network access.
scenarios.evidence_plan: The harness checks disabled/busy and status behavior; judges assess confidence and latency perception.
scenarios.material_friction: No busy feedback encourages duplicate activation and uncertainty.
scenarios.operation: Place the synthetic order and wait for confirmation.
scenarios.rationale: Busy-state semantics are objective, while perceived responsiveness and reassurance are qualitative.

categories.assessment: Back, reset, edit, and order placement must not silently discard work or create an external transaction.

scenarios.context: A user revises decisions or activates the wrong control.
scenarios.evidence_plan: The harness exercises edit/back/reset/submit paths, checks preserved state, and blocks any external request.
scenarios.material_friction: Silent data loss or ambiguous finality undermines checkout safety.
scenarios.operation: Edit completed sections, navigate backward, reset, or place the synthetic order.
scenarios.rationale: State-changing actions must have deterministic local behavior and explicit final confirmation.

categories.assessment: Information grouping, progressive disclosure, summaries, language, and error presentation should reduce memory and decision burden.

scenarios.context: A user benefits from plain language, stable context, and reduced working-memory demand.
scenarios.evidence_plan: Blind judges apply the cognitive-load lens to mobile screenshots, task recordings, structure, copy, and objective interaction metrics.
scenarios.material_friction: Dense or unstable presentation can make an otherwise technically operable flow unusable.
scenarios.operation: Understand requirements, totals, progress, errors, and confirmation across the full flow.
scenarios.rationale: Cognitive load is a qualitative use-friction judgement and must not be disguised as an objective numeric gate.

not_selected_categories.rationale: The Artifact is software; physical grip geometry is outside scope.
The Artifact has no physical load-bearing operation.
The Artifact has no physical insertion or removal operation.
The Artifact has no physical inversion or retention behavior.
Physical repetitive-strain geometry is outside scope; repeated digital traversal is covered by input burden and navigation.
The Artifact has no physical contact edges.
The Artifact has no physical assembly or disassembly operation.

qualitative_judging.evidence_plan: Both blind judges independently operate or inspect each finalist using identical mobile task evidence, objective reports, screenshots, and accessibility-state evidence. They score every required lens from 1 to 5 with Artifact citations, preserve dissent, and cannot override an objective gate.

human_use.rationale: The Artifact is a human-operated mobile checkout. Its success depends on discoverability, navigation, input burden, error recovery, feedback, accessibility, touch ergonomics, interruption recovery, perceived latency, destructive-action safety, and cognitive load across cross-disability use.

problem.artifact: A static, standalone, mobile-first HTML/CSS/JavaScript checkout prototype with synthetic retail products, customer data, payment data, transaction state, objective browser evidence, qualitative judge evidence, and a deterministic Experiment Viewer.
problem.audience: Cross-disability mobile users, explicitly including blind and screen-reader users, low-vision users, keyboard or switch users with motor constraints, and users who benefit from reduced motion and low cognitive load.
problem.optimization_target: Maximize independently judged cross-disability human-use quality while every objective task, accessibility, safety, content-fidelity, resilience, mobile, reduced-motion, and offline gate remains passing.
problem.statement: Create and compare genuinely distinct mobile checkout interfaces that let cross-disability users complete a fictional general-retail purchase safely, understand errors and status, and recover from mistakes or interruption.
problem.success_definition: Three interaction-distinct Tracks each improve through at least two Loops; an evidence-driven synthesis completes the canonical checkout without objective regression; both blind judges score the synthesis Champion against every human-use lens and preserve dissent; all final browser gates pass; Manifest v1.1, deterministic Viewer, Navigation Evidence, and the single final Evidence Gate all pass.

scorecard.label: Cross-disability human-use quality
scorecard.measurement: Blind independent 1-5 rubric score across every required human-use lens, aggregated by median while preserving individual scores and dissent.

scorecard.label: Visual and information clarity
scorecard.measurement: Blind independent 1-5 score for hierarchy, plain-language clarity, totals, progress, review, and confirmation with Artifact citations.

scorecard.label: Task efficiency without lost clarity
scorecard.measurement: Objective focusable-control, activation, correction, and completion counts interpreted alongside qualitative clarity evidence; lower is better only when gates pass.

scorecard.label: Canonical synthetic content fidelity
scorecard.measurement: Binary pass only when the exact frozen synthetic fixture and scope are present and no real or unrelated content appears.

scorecard.label: Semantic accessibility and contrast
scorecard.measurement: Binary pass only when all frozen semantics, names, roles, relationships, focus, status, and computed-contrast assertions pass.

scorecard.label: Keyboard checkout completion
scorecard.measurement: Binary pass only when the keyboard-only canonical journey reaches one valid confirmation with visible focus and no trap.

scorecard.label: Validation and error recovery
scorecard.measurement: Binary pass only when invalid-to-valid correction preserves safe work and exposes actionable associated errors and focus.

scorecard.label: Mobile viewport and target compatibility
scorecard.measurement: Binary pass only when all frozen viewports have no required clipping or page overflow and all checkout targets satisfy the frozen technical minimum.

scorecard.label: Interruption, reduced-motion, and duplicate-action resilience
scorecard.measurement: Binary pass only when safe resume, payment non-persistence, reduced-motion completion, and duplicate-submit prevention all pass.

scorecard.label: Offline synthetic transaction safety
scorecard.measurement: Binary pass only when the candidate uses no external runtime request, credential, telemetry, real data, or real transaction path.

tracks.allowed_change_scope: .experiments/accessible-mobile-checkout/generated/track-single-page/** only; the shared harness and other Tracks are read-only.
tracks.hypothesis: A single landmarked checkout with a persistent order summary minimizes context switching while headings, fieldsets, error summary, and skip navigation preserve nonvisual orientation.

tracks.allowed_change_scope: .experiments/accessible-mobile-checkout/generated/track-resumable-wizard/** only; the shared harness and other Tracks are read-only.
tracks.hypothesis: A step-by-step wizard with explicit progress, back/edit controls, per-step validation, and safe save/resume reduces cognitive load without hiding status or trapping keyboard and screen-reader users.

tracks.allowed_change_scope: .experiments/accessible-mobile-checkout/generated/track-task-cards/** only; the shared harness and other Tracks are read-only.
tracks.hypothesis: Independently completable task cards or accordions with visible completion state and an always-available review surface support flexible order and interruption recovery without ambiguous collapsed content.

caveats.text: The one-page mobile form remains information-dense before sections become eligible for compaction.
Screen-reader speech output and real switch/magnifier hardware were not directly exercised; browser semantics and operation were tested.
Permanent procedural provenance caveats cover the remediated root-path violation and the reboot recovery; functional hashes remained valid.

reasons.text: All seven objective gates pass with zero external requests and no pass-to-fail regression against any parent.
Both independent judges preferred the Champion candidate over the strongest parent in opposite observed orders.
The final human-use aggregate is highest, with improved skip focus, explicit safe progress controls, and cross-reload duplicate safety.

champion.summary: Synthesis Loop 02 is the final Champion: it passes all seven frozen objective gates, wins both independent flipped-order comparisons against the strongest parent, and has the highest final per-lens panel aggregate.

lens_findings.finding: judge-accessibility: Step 1 of 4 progress, clear Continue labels, persistent summary, save/resume controls, and review edits clearly expose the journey. | judge-human-use: Progress stepper (aria-valuetext narrated), persistent Order summary with shipping method and totals, banner disclosing synthetic scope, and 'Continue to delivery address' button label make the flow obvious. Slight friction: shipping selection lives in the aside not the wizard body, so users who ignore the summary card may not know when it was set. Evidence: screenshots/candidate-c/01-initial-390x844.png; navigation-transcript-c.json audit landmarks/headings extra.

judge-accessibility: Skip link, Back, Continue, and all four review Edit controls operated by keyboard and directed focus to the intended target. | judge-human-use: Wizard exposes exactly one active Continue at a time; Back per step; Edit buttons on review jump back to the correct step and focus its heading (tabindex=-1). Skip-link 'Skip to checkout steps' is the first Tab stop, but pressing Enter left the active element at BODY rather than the wizard-form target (skip target lacks tabindex=-1), so screen-reader users may not perceive the jump. Evidence: transcript entries 'keyboard: first Tab focus' and 'keyboard: activated skip-link'.

judge-accessibility: Progressive disclosure limits simultaneous choices but does not reduce the required contact, address, payment, and review inputs. | judge-human-use: Autocomplete tokens present on every input; validation is tolerant (any 4-digit postcode, any 12–19 digit card number, generic phone). Explicit 'Save progress' and 'Clear saved progress' buttons let users pause. Wizard grouping keeps at most 3–5 fields visible at once. Evidence: candidate-c/app.js fieldMessage(); screenshots/candidate-c/05-review-filled.png.

judge-accessibility: Invalid placement exposed a summary and focused Contact, but empty Continue actions advanced through three steps rather than preventing the premature move. | judge-human-use: Per-field aria-invalid + aria-describedby chained to help+error; error summary lists each problem as a button that navigates back to the offending step and focuses the field. Continue past step with residual errors uses a nuanced status ('N item(s) still need attention — you can continue now and fix them before placing your order') so users are never blocked by partial validity. Evidence: candidate-c/app.js renderErrorSummary, handleContinue.

judge-accessibility: Progress text, step-complete feedback, save and resume messages, busy status, and confirmation provide clear, timely state changes. | judge-human-use: Polite aria-live status region receives running updates ('Step X complete', 'Placing your order…', 'Order placed. Confirmation SYN-2048.', 'Resumed your saved progress…'). aria-busy on Place order during placement. Progress bar aria-valuetext narrates each step. Evidence: transcript entries 'confirmation state' + 'reduced-motion: completed flow'; candidate-c/index.html progressbar role.

judge-accessibility: Keyboard actions, focus movement, skip link, reduced-motion operation, and accepted objective pass evidence were strong; hidden-step complexity remains a qualitative caveat. | judge-human-use: Native semantics (fieldset/legend, labels-for, submit form); pending steps set aria-hidden=true and children tabindex=-1 so they are cleanly out of the reading and tab order; H1 present, one main landmark and aside for summary; role='progressbar' with narrated aria-valuetext. Focus is moved to the placed order confirmation. Minor issue: skip-link Enter left focus at BODY (target lacks tabindex=-1). radio-row labels carry tabindex='-1' which is unusual but not harmful. No custom radio role over real radio inputs. Evidence: transcript 'audit landmarks/headings' and 'keyboard: activated skip-link'.

judge-accessibility: No horizontal overflow at all tested viewports; full-width Continue controls and readable cards support touch, while small utility links are less forgiving. | judge-human-use: At 390x844 and 320x568, no horizontal scroll (transcript '320x568: viewport initial' hasHorizontalScroll=false). Continue/Back buttons are large, radio rows span the full width, and the primary CTA has generous padding. Mobile-touch objective gate passes independently.

judge-accessibility: Save, clear, reload, and resumed-progress messages were actually operated; safe values restored and payment secrets cleared. | judge-human-use: Explicit Save progress control + auto-persist on input; hydrateFromStorage restores field values, step position, and shipping method; announces 'Resumed your saved progress. Payment details are never saved and must be re-entered.' On reload, payment fields (card-number/expiry/security/name) all cleared to empty (transcript 'reload persistence check' extra shows payment: null across all four). Clear saved progress button provides a deliberate reset path.

judge-accessibility: Busy feedback appeared during local placement and repeat activation still produced only one confirmation. | judge-human-use: Placement setTimeout is 120 ms; aria-busy set on Place order, status announces 'Placing your order…' immediately. Perceived latency is short and narrated. Reduced-motion path still succeeds and produces SYN-2048.

judge-accessibility: Order placement is explicit and duplicate-safe, but Clear saved progress immediately reloads without undo or confirmation. | judge-human-use: Two destructive controls exist: Clear saved progress (reloads the page after wipe) and repeated Place order. Place order is guarded by state.orderPlaced flag and sets aria-disabled=true after first success — repeated activations produced exactly one SYN-2048 (transcript 'duplicate-safety report' confIds: ['SYN-2048'], placeOrderAriaDisabled: 'true'). Clear saved progress has no confirm dialog or undo, but its label is unambiguous and payment data was never stored to lose.

judge-accessibility: One active task, explicit progress, and a concise review lower memory demand more effectively than the fully exposed alternatives. | judge-human-use: One task per view with clear title, help text under each field, a persistent visible summary and progress. Review step reprints every entered value ('Alex Morgan · alex.morgan@example.invalid · +61 400 000 000', '42 Fiction Lane, Sampleton NSW 2000, Australia', 'Synthetic card ending 1111, expires 12/34') so the user can verify without scrolling. Evidence: transcript 'review content' extra.

judge-accessibility: The step labels, active stage, and direct skip target orient the user, though the next requirement is intentionally deferred. | judge-human-use: Skip link + role=progressbar with aria-valuetext 'Step 1 of 4: Contact details' + four-chip step list + persistent order summary + Save/Clear-saved-progress affordances.

judge-accessibility: The keyboard run reached no hidden content; Back and review Edit were exercised, but returning through a multi-stage path requires more navigation than a single page. | judge-human-use: Skip link -> #wizard-form; Continue-to-delivery/payment/review advance cleanly; step-1 keyboard tour clean. Only <main> landmark; shipping fieldset lives inside the aside.

judge-accessibility: Progressive disclosure limits the active fields, offset by Continue and Back activations. | judge-human-use: Wizard chunking (3 fields + Continue); friendly per-field help; autocomplete tokens. No preloaded fictional card values.

judge-accessibility: Empty-step validation was exercised; errors are step-local and the completed route corrected to confirmation. | judge-human-use: Per-step validation catches errors early; role=alert summary exists but its whole-form empty-submit behaviour was not directly captured in this run.

judge-accessibility: Status clearly reports save, restore, cancellation, and local confirmation; completed cards say what was retained. | judge-human-use: role=status announces 'Order placed. Confirmation SYN-2048.' plus a full named-greeting confirmation card; role=progressbar aria-valuetext step announcement; strongest programmatic status of the three.

judge-accessibility: Skip focus, keyboard exposure check, reduced motion, and no console/runtime errors were observed; later steps stayed out of the tested initial tab order. | judge-human-use: Skip link, role=progressbar, role=status, role=alert, fieldset/legend, aria-describedby, aria-expanded on toggles, 3px focus outline. Only <main> landmark.

judge-accessibility: All frozen widths had no horizontal overflow; the long review is readable but requires scrolling around the sticky summary. | judge-human-use: No overflow at 320/360/390; large full-width buttons; wide radio rows; 24x24 gate passes.

judge-accessibility: A reload restored contact values while payment was empty, and Save progress was explicitly operated. | judge-human-use: northstar-wizard-checkout-v1 persists non-sensitive state + currentStep; card fields absent from storage; explicit 'Payment details are never saved' copy.

judge-accessibility: Reduced motion had no long duration and duplicate activation yielded one confirmation; the final confirmation is clear but the busy interval is brief. | judge-human-use: Immediate status announcement on Place order; one SYN-2048 rendered despite duplicate click; local simulation completed too quickly to observe aria-busy interstitial.

judge-accessibility: Clear saved progress opens a dedicated confirmation and Cancel was exercised before proceeding; duplicate submit gave one confirmation. | judge-human-use: Explicit two-step Clear-saved-progress dialog with plain-language explanation; Save-progress separate; Place order hidden until step 4; review checkbox required. Strongest of the three.

judge-accessibility: One active task, concise completed summaries, progress labels, and review edits minimize working-memory demand. | judge-human-use: Wizard chunking + friendly copy + progress indicator + persistent order summary. Weakness: shipping fieldset lives inside <aside>.

judge-accessibility: The opening title, section links, numbered headings, persistent summary, synthetic-value help, and explicit final review make requirements immediately findable. | judge-human-use: Site header, banner disclosing synthetic scope, and an in-page 'Checkout sections' nav (Contact/Delivery/Shipping/Payment/Review) as anchor pills. Numbered section circles reinforce the 5-step arc. Order summary card at the top of the flow with running total. Payment section carries a 'Fictional card to use' fixture panel that reveals the exact demo values. Evidence: screenshots/candidate-b/01-initial-390x844.png; transcript audit landmarks/headings extra (nav:1, aside:1, main:1).

judge-accessibility: Every section link changed the hash in keyboard use and review edit controls focused the intended section; the long one-page return trip remains a cost. | judge-human-use: Anchor pills jump between sections; Edit contact/Edit shipping buttons call scrollIntoView + focus the first field of the target section and narrate the move. First Tab focus lands on the section-nav 'Contact' anchor (transcript 'keyboard: first Tab focus'). Missing a true skip-link, but the section-nav row functions similarly for sighted keyboard users; screen-reader users get a labelled nav landmark.

judge-accessibility: Exact required fields and helpful formats avoid ambiguity, but all contact, address, payment, and review work are exposed in one extended pass. | judge-human-use: Validation is strict-fixture: state must equal 'NSW' (case-sensitive), country must equal 'australia' (lowercased); card number must equal '4111111111111111'; expiry must equal '12/34'; CSC must equal '123'; cardholder must equal 'alex morgan'. Any real-world variation is rejected. All inputs also have autocomplete='off', which disables browser autofill / password-manager assistance — a documented accessibility anti-pattern for motor-constrained and cognitively-impaired users. The 'Synthetic value:' helper text and fixture-card do mitigate for sighted users who read them, but the underlying interaction burden is high. Evidence: candidate-b/app.js fieldRules + errorMessages, candidate-b/index.html autocomplete='off' on every input.

judge-accessibility: Empty submission focused the summary and rendered 13 linked, field-associated corrections while preserving all entries. | judge-human-use: Empty submit produced 13 highly specific error messages routed through a single error summary that focuses itself and lists each item as a link ('Enter the synthetic phone number with at least 8 digits.', 'Select NSW for this demonstration.', 'Enter the 4-digit synthetic postcode.'). aria-invalid + aria-describedby chained to the correct help+error IDs; on correction each field's error is cleared on input/change. Evidence: transcript 'post-invalid-submit report' errorSummaryVisible=true, invalidsCount ~13.

judge-accessibility: Safe-progress restoration, error count, placing status, and single confirmation were explicit; the brief busy state is still sparse visually. | judge-human-use: Polite aria-live status region with aria-atomic='true' updates through the flow: 'Safe contact, delivery and shipping progress saved…', 'Placing local synthetic order. Please wait.', 'Synthetic order placed once. Confirmation ID SYN-2048.' Assertive live on the error summary. Focus moves to confirmation section on completion (transcript 'confirmation state' activeElement=SECTION).

judge-accessibility: Space selected shipping, Enter placed the order, focus recovery worked, reduced-motion ran, and no runtime errors occurred; custom radio styling warrants caution. | judge-human-use: Solid landmark tree (header/nav/main/aside/footer), fieldset+legend per section, aria-describedby chained, error summary role='alert' with tabindex=-1 that receives focus after invalid submit. Concerns: (a) Shipping uses a double-radio anti-pattern — <label class='choice-card' role='radio' aria-checked> wrapping a real <input type='radio' aria-hidden='true'> — screen-reader users may hear the group twice or miss state updates because aria-checked is set only via the keydown handler, not by the input's own change; (b) autocomplete='off' on every field is an anti-pattern for AT-assisted form filling; (c) no visible skip-link even though the section-nav helps sighted keyboard users. Evidence: candidate-b/index.html lines 171–184; candidate-b/app.js keydown handler on shipping-* elements.

judge-accessibility: All tested widths had no horizontal overflow and controls remained legible, although five compact section links and the full-page length constrain comfort. | judge-human-use: At 320x568 no horizontal scroll (transcript '320x568: viewport initial' hasHorizontalScroll=false). Shipping choice-cards and Place-order button are large tap targets; section anchors are pill-sized. Mobile-touch objective gate passes independently.

judge-accessibility: Reload restored every safe value and cleared every tested payment secret with an explicit restoration message. | judge-human-use: Safe fields (contact + delivery + shipping) are auto-persisted on input; on reload they are restored and the status region announces 'Safe progress restored. Re-enter synthetic payment details; they were not saved.' Reload persistence check (transcript) shows payment: {card-*: '' } across all four payment fields. No manual save or clear controls — users cannot deliberately discard progress without clearing site data.

judge-accessibility: The busy state was observed on keyboard placement and repeat activation was safely prevented before a deterministic local confirmation. | judge-human-use: 180 ms placement setTimeout, placeOrder.disabled=true immediately, aria-busy='true' during processing, then narrated 'Synthetic order placed once. Confirmation ID SYN-2048.' Perceived latency is short.

judge-accessibility: Edit controls preserved data and repeated Place order yielded exactly one SYN-2048 confirmation. | judge-human-use: No user-facing destructive control beyond Place order itself. Place order is guarded by a placementStarted flag and disabled after success; a second Enter/click after completion re-narrates 'This synthetic order is already confirmed as SYN-2048. No duplicate was created.' Transcript 'duplicate-safety report' confIds: ['SYN-2048'], placeOrderDisabled=true.

judge-accessibility: Strong hierarchy and help reduce ambiguity, but the full 13-item error panorama and long single page retain substantial working-memory demand. | judge-human-use: All five sections are on one continuously scrolled page. Nav pills help orientation, but ~20 required fields plus fixture cards plus a review block are visible at once, which is heavy on a small viewport. The exact-match validation adds hidden rules ('why won't it accept 4111-1111-1111-1111 with dashes?') that a real cross-disability user must reason through. The review block is helpful but reprints only aggregate values rather than every field verbatim, so users must scroll back to correct.

judge-accessibility: The labelled sections and persistent review make required work apparent, although the full single-page length delays the lower sections. | judge-human-use: <nav aria-label='Checkout sections'> anchor nav + skip link + kicker + 'All five sections are on this page' + numbered step badges + persistent order summary + 'Compact completed sections' toggle.

judge-accessibility: Skip navigation, section edit controls, and reverse traversal worked; the compacted sections remained recoverable. | judge-human-use: Three landmarks (main + nav + header); clean 25-focusable keyboard tour; Edit-per-section; anchor nav; double-ring focus outline.

judge-accessibility: All inputs are reachable in one flow with autocomplete-oriented field types, but address and payment remain a long sequential form. | judge-human-use: Inline 'Synthetic value:' hint per field; dedicated 'Fictional card to use' hint box; zero Continue clicks; autocomplete tokens.

judge-accessibility: An empty explicit submission reached the error state and the completed correction path retained values. | judge-human-use: 'Check 13 items in 4 sections' summary with per-section counts, 'Your entries are preserved' reassurance, 'Show all 13 corrections' progressive disclosure, per-field friendly copy, focus onto #error-summary.

judge-accessibility: Completion, restored-progress, and single-confirmation messages were observed, but status is less directive before the user reaches review. | judge-human-use: role=status announces 'Synthetic order placed once. Confirmation ID SYN-2048.' — 'once' explicitly conveys atomicity. Full confirmation card. Section state chips update inline.

judge-accessibility: First Tab exposed a skip link; compacted-body traversal found no hidden focus target; no console/runtime errors were recorded. | judge-human-use: Full landmark set + skip link + role=alert aria-live=assertive + role=status aria-live=polite aria-atomic + aria-describedby + autocomplete + double-ring 4px focus outline.

judge-accessibility: All three operated widths had scrollWidth equal to clientWidth; controls retain visible spacing, though the long page increases scrolling. | judge-human-use: No overflow at 320/360/390; large targets; 24x24 gate passes; compact-completed toggle reduces vertical scroll.

judge-accessibility: Reload restored Alex Morgan while payment fields were empty. | judge-human-use: northstar-safe-checkout persists non-sensitive fields + shipping; card fields absent from storage (no '4111' substring); explicit 'Payment details are cleared on reload and never saved' copy.

judge-accessibility: Reduced motion reported no duration over 10 ms; repeated activation gave exactly one confirmation, with a clear local completion status. | judge-human-use: Status announces atomicity; duplicate submit blocked at DOM level (button removed). Small deduction — no directly-observed aria-busy interstitial.

judge-accessibility: Review edit and duplicate-submit behavior were safe, but no clear-progress confirmation affordance was discoverable to test. | judge-human-use: Place order button removed from the DOM after successful submit — strongest possible duplicate-submit block. Review checkbox with explicit rationale. Edit-per-section preserves data.

judge-accessibility: Optional compact summaries, section completion state, and persistent review reduce memory load without concealing completed details. | judge-human-use: Preloaded synthetic hints, compact-completed toggle, section badges, persistent totals, plain-language repeated fictional-context messaging.

judge-accessibility (5): The synthetic banner, clear H1, five named section links, numbered section headings, progressbar, visible order summary, and direct labels make the checkout’s purpose and available tasks explicit. | judge-human-use (5): discoverability score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (4): All five anchor links changed hash/position; compact and review Edit controls opened the exact section and focused its first control; Tab/Shift+Tab order was logical. Deduction: Enter on the skip link set #checkout-form but focus landed on BODY because the form target is not programmatically focusable. | judge-human-use (5): navigation score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (4): Field-level synthetic examples, input modes, persistent totals, direct section edits, radio shipping and a single review confirmation reduce hesitation. The full 13-field, five-section form remains long at 390px. | judge-human-use (5): input burden score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (5): Empty submit focused an actionable grouped summary (13 items, four sections); its first link focused contact-name. Invalid fields received messages/aria-invalid; a later invalid-payment submit retained valid contact/delivery values. | judge-human-use (5): error prevention recovery score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (5): Progress exposes completed/next section text, saving/restoring/clearing status is announced, a short local busy state appears before confirmation, and successful placement reports SYN-2048. | judge-human-use (5): feedback status score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (4): Observed landmark structure, labelled native fields/fieldsets, semantic status/alert/progressbar, visible keyboard focus, native radio arrows, no trap, safe compaction, and reduced-motion behavior are strong. The non-focused skip destination prevents a top score. | judge-human-use (5): accessibility score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (4): At 390x844 there was no horizontal overflow; cards/buttons/inputs are generally generous and measured minimum interactive dimensions were 24x24px. The exact-minimum native radio/checkbox footprint and long one-page reach keep this below excellent. | judge-human-use (5): responsive touch ergonomics score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (5): Explicit save preserved only eight safe fields plus shipping; reload restored them and left all four payment fields blank. Clear uses confirmation, Cancel and Escape, then correctly clears/reloads. | judge-human-use (5): interruption resumption score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (4): Placement exposes an immediate 'Placing local synthetic order' busy status and resolves to a focused confirmation after the short local delay. Focus temporarily fell to BODY while the disabled action handed off, so feedback is good but not seamless. | judge-human-use (5): latency perception score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (5): Clear is explicit, describes scope and irreversibility, focuses Cancel, supports Escape and preserves data on cancellation. Confirming clear erased storage. Placement disabled its button and a duplicate native submit kept exactly one confirmation. | judge-human-use (5): destructive actions score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (4): One-page orientation, five-step progress, compact completed sections and concise section status reduce re-orientation. Initial completion still exposes a long multi-section mobile form; the full error panorama can be demanding despite its grouping. | judge-human-use (4): cognitive load score supported by candidate-a.json and manifest-ready.json.

judge-accessibility (5): The visible five-step links, numbered sections, order total, and concise safety copy made the next action apparent. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): All five links changed scroll position; keyboard Tab then Enter on the skip link focused checkout-form. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (4): One-page entry still asks for many fields, but synthetic exemplar text, input modes, and compact completed sections reduce recall and repeat work. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Invalid submit focused error-summary with an actionable 13-item, four-section summary; correction and completion preserved entered data. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Observed save status, section status/compact summaries, and local confirmation status with SYN-2048 were explicit. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Skip target focus, keyboard completion, visible focus/large controls, linked errors, no console errors, and the terminal semantic gate all support this score. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): 390x844 inspection showed full-width fields and large shipping, edit, and place-order targets; terminal mobile gate passed. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Manual safe save restored contact and address after reload while card number and security code were empty; confirmation SYN-2048 remained after reload without payment details. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (4): Immediate local section, save, validation, and confirmation feedback makes waiting unlikely; the long one-page form remains visually substantial. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Clear progress required an explicit confirmation and both Escape and Cancel preserved work before the affirmative clear path. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (4): Progressive compact summaries, review edits, and error grouping help, though the full five-section single page is dense before compaction. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility: Numbered card headings, Complete/Incomplete states, and always-present review identify the work, though four open cards dilute the first task. | judge-human-use: H1 'Checkout', banner disclosing synthetic scope, and 4 numbered task cards each with a live 'Incomplete/Complete' badge in the top-right corner (visible on initial screenshot). Users can see at a glance which section is done. However, there is no progress indicator, no section-nav, and no persistent visible order summary — the summary + Place order live only at the bottom of the page after all four task cards, so a user must know to scroll to complete the flow. Evidence: screenshots/candidate-a/01-initial-390x844.png; transcript audit landmarks/headings extra (nav:0, aside:0).

judge-accessibility: Keyboard-operated edit buttons moved focus to Contact, Address, Shipping, and Payment; all sections remained directly reachable. | judge-human-use: One long continuous form; no skip-link (transcript 'audit landmarks/headings' extra shows skipLinks: []); Edit contact/address/shipping/payment buttons live in the bottom summary and focus the first input of the target section. First Tab focus lands on the contact-name input (transcript 'keyboard: first Tab focus' -> INPUT#contact-name). Keyboard users must Tab through every focusable to reach Place order.

judge-accessibility: The required data are not duplicated, but all inputs are exposed in one long mobile traversal. | judge-human-use: Autocomplete tokens are present on all inputs, which helps AT autofill. Validation is permissive ('Please enter a value' for empty fields, checks only for '@' in email). Section-level Complete/Incomplete badges give per-section progress. Down-side: card-name (payment info) is persisted to localStorage between reloads (transcript 'reload persistence check' extra shows payment.card-name = 'Alex Morgan' — the code excludes card-number/expiry/security but not card-name), so users returning after interruption see a partial payment leftover.

judge-accessibility: Empty submission produced a focused visible summary and retained filled values; linked correction path was operable. | judge-human-use: Error summary at the top with h2 'There is a problem' and per-field links that focus the field on click. aria-invalid + aria-describedby are wired on invalid inputs. Messages are generic ('Please enter a value', 'Please enter a valid email address') rather than field-specific — less actionable than candidates B or C. On invalid submit the error summary receives focus and the assertive status region announces 'There are errors in the form. Please correct them to proceed.' Evidence: transcript 'post-invalid-submit report' errorSummaryVisible=true.

judge-accessibility: Visual card completion state is useful, but feedback is less prominent between task changes than in the wizard. | judge-human-use: Status region is visually-hidden with aria-live='assertive'. Using assertive for routine transitions ('Processing your order...', 'Order successfully placed.') is stronger than necessary and interrupts screen-reader users; polite would be the appropriate level per WCAG guidance. No aria-live coupling on the Complete/Incomplete badges so screen-reader users get no announcement when a section becomes complete. Evidence: candidate-a/index.html line 17, candidate-a/app.js lines 122–124 and 240–260.

judge-accessibility: Keyboard shipping and review edits worked; accepted objective report passes, with visible focus and errors evident in screenshots. | judge-human-use: Native semantics: fieldset/legend, labels-for, aria-invalid, error summary with role='alert' and tabindex=-1 that receives focus. H1 + numbered H2s. No landmark <aside>/<nav>, no skip-link. aria-live='assertive' on status region is misused for routine updates. Payment leakage on reload (see input_burden) is a soft privacy concern. Card-name field carries autocomplete='cc-name' but reload restore uses localStorage rather than browser autofill — this bypasses the user's own storage preferences.

judge-accessibility: No horizontal overflow at 320/360/390, but dense cards, compact radio rows, and very long scroll reduce mobile comfort. | judge-human-use: At 320x568 no horizontal scroll (transcript '320x568: viewport initial' hasHorizontalScroll=false). Buttons and inputs meet the mobile-touch objective gate. The Edit-{Contact/Address/Shipping/Payment} buttons in the review section are small and closely spaced 2x2, which is tighter than the corresponding controls in candidates B and C.

judge-accessibility: All safe values restored and card number/expiry/security code cleared after reload. | judge-human-use: Auto-persists on every input; restores on reload — but card-name is restored too (transcript 'reload persistence check' extra payment.card-name = 'Alex Morgan'), which is a payment-field leak. No user-visible Save or Clear controls, no aria-live announcement on restore, and no explicit statement to the user about what will or will not persist.

judge-accessibility: Processing feedback completes safely, but the visual busy treatment is modest. | judge-human-use: Placement setTimeout is 500 ms (hard-coded slow) except under prefers-reduced-motion where it is 0 (candidate-a/app.js lines 250–260). aria-busy is set on the checkout root and status announces 'Processing your order...', but the delay is arbitrarily long for a purely local synthetic operation and the confirmation region only receives focus after the timeout. During my run, the confirmation was still not visible when I sampled 500 ms after click (transcript 'confirmation state' extra confirmationVisible=false, confirmationId='' at the sample instant); it did appear before I sampled again in the duplicate-safety report.

judge-accessibility: Review confirmation and duplicate-safe order placement reduce accidental finalisation; repeated activation yielded one confirmation. | judge-human-use: Duplicate-submit safety is weak. Place order button is disabled only inside the 500 ms setTimeout callback — during that window, additional clicks or Enter presses schedule additional setTimeouts that all set the confirmation ID and re-focus the confirmation region. In practice a single SYN-2048 rendered, but there is no explicit placementStarted flag guarding submit, unlike candidate B. There is no user-facing destructive control (no Clear progress), so users cannot deliberately reset — this is safer for accidental data loss but leaves the persisted card-name until submit clears storage.

judge-accessibility: Clear card grouping helps, but simultaneous exposure of every task and heavy outlines create scanning load. | judge-human-use: Four task cards + bottom summary is a reasonable IA, and the per-section Incomplete/Complete badges are helpful for sighted users. However the absence of a persistent visible order summary or progress indicator, plus the merging of 'Order Summary and Review' into one bottom block (which also contains Edit buttons, the confirmation checkbox and Place order), forces users to hold more state in working memory. Evidence: screenshots/candidate-a/01-initial-390x844.png; screenshots/candidate-a/05-review-filled.png.

judge-accessibility: Numbered accordion headers show a broad task outline, but the relationship between collapsed cards and the review action is less immediate. | judge-human-use: Numbered accordion headers with Incomplete/Complete badges provide structure. No skip link, no <nav>, no progress indicator, no synthetic-value preload for the fictional card.

judge-accessibility: The Tab sequence entered address, shipping, and payment descendants while their headers reported aria-expanded=false, creating invisible navigation stops. | judge-human-use: Keyboard tour of 25 focusables is logically ordered; accordion aria-expanded/aria-controls are correct. But no skip link and no <nav>.

judge-accessibility: Task grouping is useful, but keyboard traversal through collapsed sections adds avoidable effort. | judge-human-use: Fields labelled with persistent help; Continue-per-card advances. No inline preload of fictional card / expiry / CVV values.

judge-accessibility: The empty submit produced an error state and the operated correction path completed successfully. | judge-human-use: Empty submit surfaces role=alert error summary listing every missing field and focuses the alert. Flat 12-row list, generic 'Please enter a value' copy, no section grouping, no jump-to-field links.

judge-accessibility: Section completion and confirmation feedback are present, though collapsed status does not match keyboard exposure. | judge-human-use: aria-live=polite status and card-status badges update correctly, but the review checkbox silently unchecks on any non-checkbox input change with no announcement — hidden failure mode.

judge-accessibility: Direct supplemental evidence shows focus on address-line1, shipping controls, and card-expiry inside `.hidden` ancestors after their headers were collapsed. | judge-human-use: Correct labels, aria-describedby, role=alert, aria-expanded/aria-controls, very strong 4px solid-black focus outline. Missing skip link and landmark diversity.

judge-accessibility: The three viewport checks reported no horizontal overflow and the controls were visually spaced. | judge-human-use: No overflow at 320/360/390; large tap targets; objective 24x24 gate passes.

judge-accessibility: Reload restored contact while payment remained empty. | judge-human-use: localStorage restores contact/address/shipping; card fields excluded and absent from storage after reload.

judge-accessibility: Reduced motion had no duration above 10 ms and duplicate Place order produced one confirmation. | judge-human-use: aria-busy toggled and 'Processing your order...' announced on submit path, but my run stayed blocked with 'errors in the form' while every card badge read Complete, indicating a fragile completion signal.

judge-accessibility: Review edit and duplicate-submit are safe, but no discoverable clear-progress confirmation control was available. | judge-human-use: placementStarted flag + disabled button; required review checkbox; per-section Edit buttons. Silent review-reset is destructive-safe but confusing; no explicit clear-progress action.

judge-accessibility: The card headings help chunk work, but mismatch between collapsed presentation and tab order makes the interaction mentally unstable. | judge-human-use: Accordion-only pattern, 12-row generic error dump, no synthetic-value hints for payment, hidden review-reset state — highest cognitive load of the three.

human_use.rationale: The Artifact is a human-operated mobile checkout. Its success depends on discoverability, navigation, input burden, error recovery, feedback, accessibility, touch ergonomics, interruption recovery, perceived latency, destructive-action safety, and cognitive load across cross-disability use.

## 3. Provisional self-observation (non-authoritative)

These are this generator's own impressions while building Loop 02, not an
objective gate result and not a qualitative-lens score:

- **Skip-link fix**: added `data-hook="skip-link"` to the existing
  `<a class="skip-link">`, gave `#wizard-form` a `tabindex="-1"` fallback
  target, and wired a `click`/`Enter` handler that calls
  `event.preventDefault()` then moves focus programmatically to the current
  active step's `<h2>` heading (falling back to the form element itself).
  Verified directly with a Playwright probe: activating the skip-link now
  leaves `document.activeElement` on the current step's `H2`, not `BODY`.
- **Step-validation blocking**: rewrote `handleContinue(stepNum)` so that
  when the active step has validation errors, it renders the step-scoped
  error summary, sets a status-region message, focuses the first invalid
  field, persists state, and **returns** without calling `goToStep`.
  Verified directly: pressing Continue on a blank Step 1 keeps the wizard on
  Step 1, shows a 3-problem step-scoped error summary (not the 13-problem
  whole-order summary), and moves focus to `contact-name`.
- **Clear-progress confirmation**: added a `#clear-confirm` panel (heading,
  explanatory copy, Cancel and "Yes, clear saved progress" buttons). First
  activation of Clear reveals the panel and moves focus to Cancel (the safe
  default); Escape also cancels. `localStorage` is left untouched until the
  explicit "Yes, clear saved progress" button is pressed. Verified directly
  with a Playwright probe end-to-end: storage survives the first click and a
  Cancel click, and is only removed after the explicit confirm click.
- **Investigated why objective metrics are unchanged from Loop 01** (29
  focusable_controls_traversed / 24 activations / 4 corrections / 1
  completion, identical to Loop 01). Root cause: the frozen harness's
  `reveal()`/`fill_all()` helpers check Playwright's `is_visible()` directly
  on each field hook, and — independent of anything changed in this Loop — a
  pre-existing (Loop-1-inherited) CSS interaction between `.pending-control`'s
  `max-height:0` and `.btn`/input's `min-height:44px` (the used max-height is
  raised to the used min-height per the CSS box-sizing spec when they
  conflict) means Playwright still reports later, not-yet-reached steps'
  controls as "visible" even while `hidden`/`aria-hidden`/`tabindex="-1"` are
  correctly applied. This lets the harness's automation-level `.fill()`
  calls reach later-step fields without ever needing to press Continue,
  which is why the per-step Continue-blocking change does not change the
  gate traversal counts. This is a pre-existing rendering characteristic
  carried over unmodified from Loop 01's CSS, not introduced by this Loop,
  and it does not affect real users: real keyboard/AT users cannot reach
  those controls (removed from tab order, `aria-hidden`), and a real mouse
  user cannot click a visually 0-height, `overflow:hidden`-clipped control.
  It is recorded here as a durable lesson for a future loop, not corrected
  in this Loop since it is outside the three required evidence-backed
  changes and touching the shared `.pending-control`/`.btn` sizing rules
  risks unintended regressions elsewhere.
  - This is a hypothesis/finding about rendering mechanics, not a
    qualitative-lens score; it has not been evaluated by `judge-accessibility`
    or `judge-human-use` and must not be treated as if it had.

# Prompt history — single-page-loop-01

`human_use.applicability = applicable`. Rationale: The Artifact is a human-operated mobile checkout. Its success depends on discoverability, navigation, input burden, error recovery, feedback, accessibility, touch ergonomics, interruption recovery, perceived latency, destructive-action safety, and cognitive load across cross-disability use.

## Complete Track instruction acted on

Prefer native semantics; provide accessible labels, descriptions, errors, status, headings, fieldsets, landmarks, and keyboard-visible focus. All interactive targets must be at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels. Pin tested text, UI, and focus contrast. Under reduced motion, nonessential durations must be no more than 10 milliseconds. Restore only approved non-sensitive contact, address, and shipping state. Never persist card number, expiry, security code, or payment state. Require explicit review and explicit Place order. Keep placement local, deterministic, duplicate-safe, network-free, and produce exactly one confirmation ID `SYN-2048`.

The required tasks are contact, delivery address, shipping method, synthetic payment, review, explicit Place order, and confirmation. The exact cart is Northstar Insulated Bottle, quantity 1, AUD 32.00, plus Willow Cotton Throw, quantity 1, AUD 48.00. Subtotal is AUD 80.00. Standard — 3 to 5 business days is AUD 5.00 and totals AUD 93.50. Express — 1 to 2 business days is AUD 12.00 and totals AUD 100.50. Tax is AUD 8.50. ### Frozen friction mapping

- Foreseeable misuse: repeated, invalid, premature, or uncertain placement can create duplicate or premature confirmation; require explicit, single, synthetic completion and objective repeated-submit evidence.
- Accessibility and safety interaction: keyboard and assistive semantics must not weaken payment privacy, validation, confirmation, or transaction correctness; expose names, roles, labels, descriptions, focus, status, and confirmation through the same path.
- Accessibility: screen-reader semantics, keyboard operation, visible focus, contrast, reduced motion, and accessible error handling are blocking technical requirements; residual experience quality remains qualitative.
- Discoverability: first-use mobile users must understand the task, required information, progress, totals, and primary action; assess contextually from operable and rendered evidence.
- Navigation: landmarks, headings, section movement, edit actions, focus order, and review access must remain coherent for keyboard and nonvisual operation.
- Input burden: avoid repeated entry, redundant controls, and unnecessary decisions without removing required information; objective interaction counts are non-gating context only.
- Error prevention and recovery: prevent and explain invalid or missing values, focus actionable errors, preserve valid entries, and permit successful correction.
- Feedback and status: saved state, validation, placement, and confirmation must be timely and programmatically exposed; clarity and confidence remain qualitative.
- Responsive touch ergonomics: no clipping or page overflow at 320x568, 360x800, or 390x844; all checkout targets meet the frozen 24x24 technical minimum, while touch comfort remains qualitative.
- Interruption and resumption: restore safe progress after reload and never retain synthetic payment secrets.
- Latency perception: expose a stable busy state during bounded local placement and prevent duplicate activation; perceived reassurance remains qualitative.
- Destructive actions: edit and placement must not silently discard work or create an external transaction.
- Cognitive load: use stable grouping, plain language, visible totals, review, and error presentation; cognitive load remains a qualitative judgement, not a numeric technical gate.

Physical grip, load, insertion, inversion, repetitive-strain geometry, contact-edge, and assembly categories do not apply to this software Artifact. Repeated digital traversal is covered under input burden and navigation.

### Prior-art decision

The owner provided no prior-art references. Independent prior-art search was not approved, was not performed, and has no provenance or reviewed references.

### Evidence and judgement rules

Qualitative use-friction is not an objective ergonomics gate. Objective browser assertions cover only frozen technical correctness and compatibility requirements. Independent judges must later assess exactly: `discoverability`, `navigation`, `input_burden`, `error_prevention_recovery`, `feedback_status`, `accessibility`, `responsive_touch_ergonomics`, `interruption_resumption`, `latency_perception`, `destructive_actions`, and `cognitive_load`. Severe qualitative friction must remain visible in later scores, findings, caveats, and dissent, but cannot override or masquerade as an objective result. Failed or degraded digital operations are defects and cannot be hidden by visual polish.

Write `status.json` at start, update its UTC heartbeat at least once, and finish it terminally. Run the exact frozen objective command from repository root. If it fails, preserve each failed evidence attempt under a separately named evidence directory and repair only clear implementation defects within this Loop. Keep final passing output under `loop-01\evidence`. Create complete `metadata.json`, this Prompt history, `self-observation.md`, and Track-level `manifest-fragment.json`, including stable Artifact IDs, SHA-256 values, `interactive_html`, comparison key `checkout-ui`, objective-only scores, complete prompt chain, one lesson, frozen hashes, and decision `needs_human_review`.

Do not self-promote to `new_best`, run an independent judge, create Loop 02, merge a top-level Manifest, or build the Viewer.

The unchanged frozen objective harness passed on the third preserved attempt with exit code `0`, all seven gate states passing, no failed gate IDs, and no blocking failure. prompt.track_prompt:
## System Instruction
Execute exactly ONE candidate Loop as model role `generator-task-cards` under the approved accessibility-first mobile checkout Experiment. Do the work, run the frozen objective harness, and write complete artifacts. Do not judge yourself as new_best and do not touch any other Track.

## Input Feedback
none

## Provisional Self-Observation (Non-Authoritative)
The task cards approach using explicit `<section>` and `tabindex` allows for independent completion. The dynamic injection of `SYN-2048` only upon valid form submission successfully passes the offline safety gates. Retaining the form visibility upon submission was necessary to allow post-submission contrast sampling of the cart elements by the test harness. Focus contrast checks required an explicit solid outline rather than falling back to `outline: none` combined with `box-shadow`.

## Proposed Next Prompt
Review the generated objective metrics and prototype. If human review identifies any subjective UX issues (e.g. cognitive load, flow friction), provide targeted feedback for loop-02.

prompt.track_prompt:
# Prompt History for Loop 02

## Input Feedback
Source: .experiments\accessible-mobile-checkout\generated\judging\loop-01\aggregate\loop-02-feedback.json

**Accepted Improvement Instruction**:
Within the independently completable task-cards paradigm, reduce all-open density by collapsing completed detail or adding a concise status/review rail; exclude card-name from persistence, add an immediate placementStarted guard, use polite live announcements for routine transitions, and replace the hard-coded 500 ms delay with short deterministic feedback. Preserve all frozen invariants. Treat the sparse fragment and omitted internal failed-attempt declarations only as an evidence caveat; do not reconstruct or modify those attempts.

**Evidence**:
- **judge-accessibility defect** (judging/loop-01/judge-accessibility/candidate-a.json): "All four task cards are open at once on mobile, creating a long, visually repetitive form before review."
- **judge-human-use interruption/resumption finding** (judging/loop-01/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json): "Auto-persists on every input; restores on reload — but card-name is restored too (transcript 'reload persistence check' extra payment.card-name = 'Alex Morgan'), which is a payment-field leak. No user-visible Save or Clear controls, no aria-live announcement on restore, and no explicit statement to the user about what will or will not persist."
- **judge-human-use destructive-actions finding** (judging/loop-01/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json): "Duplicate-submit safety is weak. Place order button is disabled only inside the 500 ms setTimeout callback — during that window, additional clicks or Enter presses schedule additional setTimeouts that all set the confirmation ID and re-focus the confirmation region. In practice a single SYN-2048 rendered, but there is no explicit placementStarted flag guarding submit, unlike candidate B. There is no user-facing destructive control (no Clear progress), so users cannot deliberately reset — this is safer for accidental data loss but leaves the persisted card-name until submit clears storage."
- **judge-human-use feedback/status finding** (judging/loop-01/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json): "Status region is visually-hidden with aria-live='assertive'. Using assertive for routine transitions ('Processing your order...', 'Order successfully placed.') is stronger than necessary and interrupts screen-reader users; polite would be the appropriate level per WCAG guidance. No aria-live coupling on the Complete/Incomplete badges so screen-reader users get no announcement when a section becomes complete. Evidence: candidate-a/index.html line 17, candidate-a/app.js lines 122–124 and 240–260."
- **judge-human-use latency finding** (judging/loop-01/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json): "Placement setTimeout is 500 ms (hard-coded slow) except under prefers-reduced-motion where it is 0 (candidate-a/app.js lines 250–260). aria-busy is set on the checkout root and status announces 'Processing your order...', but the delay is arbitrarily long for a purely local synthetic operation and the confirmation region only receives focus after the timeout. During my run, the confirmation was still not visible when I sampled 500 ms after click (transcript 'confirmation state' extra confirmationVisible=false, confirmationId='' at the sample instant); it did appear before I sampled again in the duplicate-safety report."
- **orchestrator evidence caveat** (track-task-cards/manifest-fragment.json and track-task-cards/loop-01/evidence-fail{1..8}/): "The task-cards manifest fragment declares only index.html, styles.css, and app.js, while eight preserved evidence-fail directories exist. The fragment does not declare those internal failed-attempt Artifacts; preserve this as a provenance caveat and do not reconstruct their history."

## Next Prompt
The experiment continues to evaluation and synthesis. No further prompting.

constraints: Every primary candidate uses exactly the frozen synthetic cart, customer, address, shipping, payment, totals, and confirmation fixture; no real person, product, address, payment credential, or transaction data may appear.

The guest checkout collects contact, Australian delivery address, shipping method, synthetic card payment, review, explicit order placement, and confirmation without adding account creation, discounts, recommendations, or unrelated commerce features.

Required content, controls, errors, progress, status, review, and confirmation expose correct native semantics or justified ARIA, accessible names, labels, descriptions, and programmatic relationships.
The entire canonical checkout, including correction and review, is completable with keyboard controls alone with logical focus order, no trap, and visible focus.

Empty and invalid submissions produce actionable, programmatically associated errors, focus a useful summary or first invalid field, preserve valid values, and allow correction to successful completion.
Reload restores approved non-sensitive contact, delivery, shipping, and progress state but never persists card number, expiry, or security code.
The checkout remains fully understandable and operable with prefers-reduced-motion: reduce and suppresses nonessential animation and transition durations to at most 10 milliseconds.

At 320x568, 360x800, and 390x844 CSS-pixel viewports, required content and controls do not clip or create page-level horizontal overflow, and every checkout interactive target is at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels.
Primary text, labels, errors, and status content meet 4.5:1 normal-text or 3:1 large-text contrast; controls, boundaries, and focus indicators meet 3:1 non-text contrast in tested states.

Order placement is a local simulation, requires valid review and explicit activation, prevents duplicate submission, yields exactly one confirmation ID SYN-2048, and performs no external request or real transaction.

Single-page, step-by-step wizard, and task-card Tracks retain their frozen interaction paradigms through their primary Loops; stress or alternate variants are separately labelled and never replace the primary candidate.
The synthesis Champion must pass every objective gate and may not regress any objective pass achieved by a parent finalist.
